text.title: Traceability Auto-Maintenance — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Traceability Auto-Maintenance must enforce.

rule.statement: The traceability graph rebuilds daily (≤24h freshness).
rule.rationale: Graphs older than a day mislead engineers and BAs into decisions on stale links.

rule.statement: Inputs come from systems-of-record (ticket-system, git, CI), not from spreadsheets or copies.
rule.rationale: Derived data drifts from reality; only SoR inputs guarantee accuracy.

rule.statement: Alerts on broken links route to a single named owner with an SLA.
rule.rationale: Anonymous alerts go to a shared channel and are ignored.

rule.statement: If any connector fails, the job emits 'graph stale' with the failure cause; no silent fallback to yesterday's data.
rule.rationale: Silent fallback means the team trusts a snapshot that may be days old.

rule.statement: The graph artefact carries a schema_version that bumps on every schema change.
rule.rationale: Consumers downstream break silently on schema drift; explicit version prevents this.
